behaviors.description: The traveling-salesman heuristic produces a route that is no more than 1.5x the length of the optimal route for inputs up to 20 stops.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is the heuristic algorithm’s output quality versus a reference optimum, which can be exercised in-process with fixed coordinate sets and a reference optimal-length oracle dataset. No real OS/network/subprocess or AI dependency is required. Higher level rejected: Integration/System are disqualified because behavior text is about route-quality math, not module handoff or infrastructure effects.
behaviors.test_description: Call the heuristic function directly on curated stop sets (including edge shapes and adversarial layouts), compare returned route length to precomputed optimal length for each case, and fail if ratio exceeds 1.5.

behaviors.description: The delivery time window estimates are computed correctly from the distance between consecutive stops and the configured average speed for the road type.
behaviors.justification: This is deterministic formula logic (time = distance/speed with road-type speed lookup) and can be validated at a single function/object boundary with in-memory config. Higher level rejected: Integration is disqualified because behavior text does not require external config store, routing service, or infrastructure boundary to reveal the failure.
behaviors.test_description: Invoke the estimator with explicit segment distances, road types, and speed config; assert per-leg and total window outputs, including rounding and mixed road-type cases.

behaviors.description: The shift-hours validator rejects a route whose total estimated time exceeds the driver's remaining shift hours, returning the excess amount.
behaviors.justification: Behavior is pure validation arithmetic and threshold handling on provided totals/hours; no cross-module or infra dependency is needed to catch real failures. Higher level rejected: Integration/System are disqualified because the described failure is local decision logic plus excess-time computation.
behaviors.test_description: Call validator with route-time and remaining-shift inputs across boundary conditions (equal, just over, far over) and assert rejection flag plus exact excess amount.

behaviors.description: When one address in the list fails geocoding (API returns no results), the service skips that stop and optimizes the remaining addresses, reporting which addresses were skipped.
behaviors.justification: Real failure appears at module handoff: geocoding result handling -> stop filtering -> optimizer input -> skip-report output. A deterministic fake geocoder returning "no results" is sufficient; real network behavior is not required by this behavior text. Higher level rejected: System is disqualified because text specifies semantic API response content (no results), not transport failures/timeouts/real HTTP effects.
behaviors.test_description: Wire service + geocoder client adapter (stubbed in-process) + optimizer module; feed addresses where one geocode response is empty; assert optimized route excludes that stop and response includes the exact skipped address list.

behaviors.description: Duplicate addresses in the input list are consolidated into a single stop with combined delivery items, not visited twice.
behaviors.justification: Failure is likely at interaction boundary between deduplication/aggregation and route construction (ensuring merged payload and single visit in final route), not just one local helper. No real OS/network/AI is needed. Higher level rejected: System is disqualified because behavior text is in-process data normalization and planner input/output consistency.
behaviors.test_description: Run service pipeline with duplicate-address inputs carrying distinct item lists; assert one consolidated stop in planner input/output, merged items are preserved, and final route contains that address exactly once.
